steps.title: Evaluate the function at x = 0
steps.content:
The y-intercept occurs where $x = 0$. For $f(x) = -10(3)^x + \frac{1}{k}$, substitute $x = 0$:
$$f(0) = -10(3)^0 + \frac{1}{k}$$
Since $3^0 = 1$:
$$f(0) = -10(1) + \frac{1}{k} = -10 + \frac{1}{k}$$

steps.title: Combine into a single fraction
steps.content:
Express both terms with denominator $k$:
$$-10 + \frac{1}{k} = \frac{-10k}{k} + \frac{1}{k} = \frac{-10k + 1}{k} = \frac{1 - 10k}{k}$$
That confirms B is correct.
